Acetazolamide.notes: 8-30 mg/kg/day divided q6-12h. Max 1 g/day. Sustained-release capsules NOT for seizures.

Acetazolamide.duration_note: Until shunt placed or hydrocephalus resolves
Acetazolamide.notes: Start 20 mg/kg/day divided q8h PO/IV. Increase to max 100 mg/kg/day. Often combined with furosemide 1 mg/kg/day. Monitor electrolytes and metabolic acidosis.

Amitriptyline.notes: Start 10 mg at bedtime; increase weekly by 0.25 mg/kg if tolerated. Max 1 mg/kg/day. Takes 4-8 weeks for effect. Anticholinergic side effects common.
Give 1-2 hours before bedtime. Ages 6-12 y: 10-25 mg. Ages 12-18 y: 25-50 mg. ECG before starting. Avoid in cardiac conduction defects.
Start low (0.1 mg/kg) at bedtime. Increase slowly every 1-2 weeks. Monitor for sedation, weight gain, QTc prolongation.

Aripiprazole.duration_note: Ongoing; reassess periodically for lowest effective dose
Aripiprazole.notes: FDA approved ages 6-17. Start 2 mg/day, increase to 5 mg after minimum 1 week, then 10-15 mg if needed. Monitor metabolic parameters, weight, EPS.
FDA approved ages 10-17. Start 2 mg/day x2 days, then 5 mg/day x2 days, target 10 mg/day. May increase by 5 mg increments to max 30 mg/day.
FDA approved ages 13-17. Start 2 mg x2d, then 5 mg x2d, target 10 mg. Max 30 mg/day; 30 mg not more efficacious than 10 mg.

Atomoxetine.duration_note: Increase to target after minimum 3 days
Atomoxetine.notes: Start 0.5 mg/kg/day for minimum 3 days before increasing. Can give once daily AM or split AM and late PM.
Target 1.2 mg/kg/day; max 1.4 mg/kg/day or 100 mg/day, whichever is less. Non-stimulant; takes 4-6 weeks for full effect. Monitor growth, HR, BP.

Baclofen.duration_note: Titrate every 3 days to target dose over weeks
Baclofen.notes: Start 2.5 mg TDS. Titrate by 5 mg every 3 days. Target 10-15 mg/day. Max 40 mg/day for ages 2-7. Do NOT stop abruptly — risk of withdrawal seizures.

Baclofen.duration_note: Ongoing; adjust based on spasticity control
Baclofen.notes: Ages 8-11: 10-40 mg/day. Ages >12: 20-60 mg/day. Max 80 mg/day. Gradual titration over weeks. Never abruptly discontinue.

Botulinum toxin A.duration_note: Repeat every 3-6 months as needed; single session dosing
Botulinum toxin A.notes: Botox (onabotulinumtoxinA): 4-12 U/kg per session, max 300 U total per session. For multi-level injections, up to 6-28 U/kg. NOT interchangeable with Dysport. Limit to <18 U/kg in severe quadriplegia with dysphagia.

Carbamazepine.duration_note: Titrate weekly over 4-6 weeks to maintenance
Carbamazepine.notes: Start 5-10 mg/kg/day in 2 divided doses. Increase by 5 mg/kg/day weekly. Check HLA-B*15:02 before starting (Stevens-Johnson risk in South Asians). Autoinduction of metabolism over 2-4 weeks.
Maintenance 10-30 mg/kg/day divided BD-TDS. Children <6y may need TDS. Therapeutic level 4-12 mcg/mL. Max 35 mg/kg/day. Children >12y: 800-1200 mg/day. Monitor CBC, LFT, Na.

Clonazepam.duration_note: Start low; titrate every 3 days. Ongoing maintenance.
Clonazepam.notes: Start 0.01-0.03 mg/kg/day divided BD-TDS (max initial 0.05 mg/kg/day). Increase by 0.01-0.02 mg/kg every 3 days. Maintenance 0.1-0.2 mg/kg/day. Max 0.2 mg/kg/day. Risk of tolerance; drowsiness/hyperactivity in children.

Clonidine.notes: Start 0.05 mg at bedtime, increase by 0.05 mg weekly. Usual range 0.15-0.3 mg/day in 2-3 divided doses. Max 0.4 mg/day. ER formulation: 0.1-0.4 mg/day BD. Monitor HR, BP. Do NOT stop abruptly.

Deflazacort.notes: 0.9 mg/kg/day once daily (FDA-approved for DMD age >=2y). Give in the morning with food. Monitor for Cushingoid features, growth, glucose, bone density, cataracts. Adrenal suppression risk — do NOT stop abruptly.

Diazepam.duration_note: Single dose; may repeat once after 10 min if seizure persists
Diazepam.notes: Rectal diazepam 0.5 mg/kg (max 10 mg). Repeat once after 10 min if needed. Preferred rectal route when no IV access.

Diazepam.duration_note: Single dose; may repeat once after 5 min
Diazepam.notes: IV diazepam 0.15-0.3 mg/kg (max 10 mg). Give slowly over 2-5 min. May repeat once after 5 min. Risk of respiratory depression — have resuscitation equipment ready.
0.1-0.3 mg/kg/dose PO q6-8h. Short-term use only. Risk of paradoxical excitation in young children.

Duloxetine.notes: FDA approved for GAD ages 7-17. Start 30 mg/day, may increase to 60-120 mg/day. FDA BLACK BOX WARNING: increased risk of suicidal thinking/behavior in children and young adults <25y. Close monitoring required in first weeks.

Gabapentin.duration_note: Titrate over 3-7 days to maintenance; ongoing
Gabapentin.notes: Start 10-15 mg/kg/day TDS. Increase by 10 mg/kg/day every 1-3 days. Maintenance: 25-40 mg/kg/day (age 3-4y: up to 50 mg/kg/day; age 5-12y: 25-35 mg/kg/day). Max 50 mg/kg/day or 3600 mg/day.
Start 300 mg TDS. Increase by 300 mg/day every 1-3 days. Usual maintenance 900-1200 mg/day. Max 3600 mg/day.
Start 5 mg/kg/day TDS, escalate every 2-3 days. Usual range 8-35 mg/kg/day. Max 70 mg/kg/day per St. Jude guidelines; max 3600 mg/day absolute.

Ibuprofen.duration_note: As needed; max 5 days without medical review
Ibuprofen.notes: 5-10 mg/kg/dose q6-8h. Max 40 mg/kg/day or 1200 mg/day. NOT for infants <6 months. Give with food. Avoid in dehydration, renal impairment, chickenpox.
30-40 mg/kg/day in 3-4 divided doses for JIA. Higher doses than fever/pain use. Monitor GI side effects, renal function.

Imipramine.notes: Start 0.5 mg/kg at bedtime, increase gradually. Max 2.5 mg/kg/day. Ages 6-12: 10-25 mg at bedtime; >12y: 25-50 mg. ECG before starting. Keep out of children's reach — TCA overdose is fatal.

Lamotrigine.indication: Epilepsy — initiation (NOT on valproate, NOT on enzyme inducers)
Lamotrigine.duration_note: Weeks 1-2: 0.3 mg/kg/day; Weeks 3-4: 0.6 mg/kg/day; then increase by 0.6 mg/kg every 1-2 weeks
Lamotrigine.notes: VERY SLOW titration to reduce Stevens-Johnson syndrome risk (0.3-0.8% in pediatric). Wk1-2: 0.3 mg/kg/day OD; Wk3-4: 0.6 mg/kg/day; then increase by 0.6 mg/kg q1-2wk.
Maintenance 5-15 mg/kg/day in 1-2 divided doses. Max 400 mg/day. WITH valproate: halve the dose at every stage. WITH enzyme inducers (CBZ, PHT): double the dose.
When on valproate: halve all lamotrigine doses. Wk1-2: 0.15 mg/kg/day; Wk3-4: 0.3 mg/kg/day. Maintenance 1-5 mg/kg/day, max 200 mg/day. SJS risk higher with valproate co-therapy.

Levetiracetam.duration_note: Increase by 10-20 mg/kg/day every 2 weeks to maintenance
Levetiracetam.notes: Start 10 mg/kg BD (20 mg/kg/day). Increase by 10 mg/kg BD every 2 weeks. Behavioral side effects (irritability) common in children — consider pyridoxine 50 mg/day.
Maintenance 40-60 mg/kg/day divided BD. Max 3000 mg/day. No drug interactions (not hepatically metabolized). Can be given IV at same dose if unable to take PO.

Levetiracetam.duration_note: Start 7 mg/kg BD, increase by 7 mg/kg BD every 2 weeks
Levetiracetam.notes: 1-6 months: start 7 mg/kg BD, target 21 mg/kg BD. 6m-4y: start 10 mg/kg BD, target 25 mg/kg BD.
IV loading dose 40-60 mg/kg (max 3000 mg) over 15 min for status epilepticus. Follow with maintenance 30 mg/kg BD.

Levodopa/carbidopa.duration_note: Titrate gradually over weeks to response
Levodopa/carbidopa.notes: Dose refers to LEVODOPA component. Start 1 mg/kg/day divided TDS. Most DRD patients respond to 4-5 mg/kg/day. Adequate trial: up to 20 mg/kg/day for 1 month. Use carbidopa/levodopa 25/100 mg tabs.

Levodopa/carbidopa.duration_note: Lifelong in DRD; doses remain remarkably stable
Levodopa/carbidopa.notes: Most DRD patients stabilize at 4-5 mg/kg/day levodopa. Range 0.5-20 mg/kg/day. Dramatic response in Segawa disease. Max 600 mg/day levodopa.

Lithium.duration_note: Titrate to therapeutic level; ongoing with monitoring
Lithium.notes: Start 300 mg BD-TDS based on weight. Target 30 mg/kg/day. Therapeutic level 0.8-1.2 mEq/L (acute), 0.6-1.0 mEq/L (maintenance). Toxic >1.4 mEq/L. Monitor levels, TFT, renal function, Ca. Narrow therapeutic index. Ensure adequate hydration.

Lorazepam.notes: 0.1 mg/kg IV (max 4 mg/dose). Preferred IV benzodiazepine for status epilepticus. May repeat once after 5-10 min. Monitor respiratory status.
0.02-0.05 mg/kg/dose q4-8h PO/IV. Short-term use only. Risk of paradoxical excitation in young children.

Oxcarbazepine.notes: Start 8-10 mg/kg/day divided BD. Max initial 600 mg/day. Titrate to target over 2 weeks based on weight.
Target by weight: 20-29 kg → 900 mg/day; 29-39 kg → 1200 mg/day; >39 kg → 1800 mg/day. Children <4y may need up to 60 mg/kg/day. Monitor Na — risk of hyponatremia.

Paracetamol.duration_note: As needed; max 5 days without medical review
Paracetamol.notes: 10-15 mg/kg/dose q4-6h PO. Max 60 mg/kg/day or 4 g/day. Neonates: 10-15 mg/kg q6-8h (max 40 mg/kg/day). Hepatotoxicity risk in overdose — N-acetylcysteine for >150 mg/kg ingestion.

Paracetamol.duration_note: As needed; switch to oral when possible
Paracetamol.notes: Rectal 15-20 mg/kg/dose q4-6h. Absorption slower and erratic vs oral. Use when vomiting or oral route unavailable. Available as 125 mg, 250 mg, 500 mg suppositories.

Paracetamol.duration_note: Perioperative or when oral/rectal not feasible
Paracetamol.notes: IV paracetamol 15 mg/kg/dose q6h. Infuse over 15 min. Neonates and <10 kg: 7.5 mg/kg/dose q6h (max 30 mg/kg/day). >50 kg: adult dose 1 g.

Pyridostigmine.duration_note: Ongoing; adjust based on clinical response
Pyridostigmine.notes: 1-7 mg/kg/day divided q4-6h. Neonates: 5 mg PO q4-6h (max 10 mg/dose). Increase gradually based on muscle strength. Cholinergic crisis if overdosed — have atropine available.

Pyridostigmine.duration_note: Until transient neonatal MG resolves (days to weeks)
Pyridostigmine.notes: Neonatal MG: 5 mg PO q4-6h. IM: 0.05-0.15 mg/kg q4-6h. Transient neonatal MG usually resolves in 2-8 weeks.

Riluzole.notes: Off-label/investigational. 50 mg OD provides exposure comparable to adult 50 mg BD. Monitor LFTs monthly for first 3 months, then periodically. Safety in children not formally established.

Risperidone.duration_note: Initiation dose for 4 days, then increase. Ongoing; reassess every 3 months
Risperidone.notes: FDA approved 5-16y. <20 kg: start 0.25 mg/day, increase to 0.5 mg after 4 days. >=20 kg: start 0.5 mg/day, increase to 1 mg after 4 days. Effective range 0.5-3 mg/day. Use lowest effective dose. Monitor weight gain, metabolic parameters, prolactin, EPS.

Rizatriptan.duration_note: Single dose; only 1 dose per 24 hours in pediatrics
Rizatriptan.notes: <40 kg: 5 mg single dose. >=40 kg: 10 mg single dose. Only ONE dose per 24h in pediatrics ages 6-17. ODT (orally disintegrating tablet) available.

Sertraline.duration_note: Ongoing; minimum 8-12 weeks to assess efficacy
Sertraline.notes: FDA approved for OCD ages 6-17. Start 25 mg/day; increase by 25 mg every 1-2 weeks. Target 50-150 mg/day. Max 200 mg/day. FDA BLACK BOX WARNING: increased risk of suicidal thinking/behavior in children and young adults <25y.
Start 50 mg/day in adolescents. May increase by 50 mg every 1-2 weeks. Max 200 mg/day. FDA BLACK BOX WARNING: Monitor for suicidality especially first 4 weeks and dose changes.

Escitalopram.duration_note: Ongoing; minimum 8 weeks to assess efficacy
Escitalopram.notes: FDA approved for MDD ages 12-17. Start 10 mg/day; may increase to 20 mg after minimum 3 weeks. FDA BLACK BOX WARNING: increased risk of suicidal thinking/behavior in children and young adults <25y.
Off-label for OCD/anxiety. Start 5 mg/day; increase to 10-20 mg over weeks. FDA BLACK BOX WARNING on suicidality.

Fluoxetine.notes: FDA approved for MDD ages 8-17. Start 10 mg/day; increase to 20 mg after 1 week if tolerated. ONLY SSRI FDA-approved for pediatric depression. FDA BLACK BOX WARNING on suicidality.

Fluoxetine.duration_note: Ongoing; several weeks to full effect
Fluoxetine.notes: FDA approved for OCD ages 7-17. Start 10 mg/day, increase to 20 mg after 2 weeks. Usual range 20-60 mg/day. Max 80 mg/day. FDA BLACK BOX WARNING on suicidality. Very long half-life (4-6 days active metabolite).

Fluphenazine.duration_note: Ongoing; titrate to lowest effective dose
Fluphenazine.notes: Off-label for tics/Tourette. Start 0.5-1 mg/day, increase by 0.5-1 mg weekly. Mean effective dose ~3 mg/day. Max 10 mg/day in children. Monitor for tardive dyskinesia, EPS, NMS. Not for children <12y.

Fluvoxamine.notes: FDA approved for OCD ages 8-17. Start 25 mg at bedtime; increase by 25 mg every 4-7 days. Children <=11y: max 200 mg/day. Adolescents: max 300 mg/day. Doses >50 mg should be divided BD. FDA BLACK BOX WARNING on suicidality.

Melatonin.duration_note: Give 30-60 min before bedtime; reassess periodically
Melatonin.notes: Ages 2-5y: 1-3 mg at bedtime. Start with lowest dose. Not a sedative — works on circadian rhythm. Effectiveness mainly for sleep onset latency.

Melatonin.duration_note: Give 30-60 min before bedtime; reassess every 3 months
Melatonin.notes: Ages 6-13y: 3-5 mg. ASD children may benefit from higher doses. Prolonged-release formulation available (PedPRM). Combine with sleep hygiene measures.
Adolescents: 5-10 mg. Doses >6 mg rarely show additional benefit in neurotypical adolescents. Max 10-12 mg. Available as tablet, gummy, liquid.

Methylphenidate.duration_note: Give before breakfast and lunch; titrate weekly
Methylphenidate.notes: Start 5 mg BD (breakfast + lunch). Increase by 5-10 mg/week. Usual effective 0.3-1 mg/kg before breakfast and lunch. Max 60 mg/day or 2 mg/kg/day. Drug holiday on weekends/vacations may be considered. Monitor growth, HR, BP, appetite.
ER (Concerta/equivalent): Start 18 mg every morning. Increase by 18 mg weekly. Max 54 mg/day for children 6-12y. Swallow whole — do not crush/chew.
Adolescents 13-17y: Start 18 mg every morning. Max 72 mg/day. Schedule II controlled substance.

Midazolam.duration_note: Single dose; may repeat once after 10 min
Midazolam.notes: Buccal: 0.5 mg/kg (max 10 mg). Intranasal: 0.2 mg/kg (max 10 mg). IM: 0.2 mg/kg (max 10 mg). Preferred when no IV access. 13-40 kg: 5 mg IM; >40 kg: 10 mg IM.

Midazolam.duration_note: Bolus; for refractory SE, follow with infusion
Midazolam.notes: IV bolus 0.1-0.2 mg/kg (max 10 mg). For refractory SE: continuous infusion 0.05-0.4 mg/kg/hr (max 0.84 mg/kg/hr). Titrate to EEG seizure suppression.

Midazolam.duration_note: Single dose IV; may repeat with 0.05 mg/kg
Midazolam.notes: IV: 0.05-0.1 mg/kg (max 5 mg). Oral: 0.25-0.5 mg/kg (max 20 mg) given 30-45 min before procedure. Intranasal: 0.2-0.3 mg/kg. Monitor O2 sat, have flumazenil available.

Sumatriptan.duration_note: Single dose; may repeat after 2 hours. Max 2 doses/24h
Sumatriptan.notes: Intranasal preferred in pediatrics. 20-39 kg: 10 mg; >=40 kg: 20 mg. May repeat after 2h if needed. Max 40 mg/24h. Avoid in hemiplegic or basilar migraine.

Sumatriptan.duration_note: Single dose; may repeat after 2 hours. Max 200 mg/24h
Sumatriptan.notes: Start 25 mg PO. May increase to 50-100 mg. Repeat after 2h if needed. Max 200 mg/24h. Not for prophylaxis.

Tetrabenazine.notes: Off-label in children. Start 12.5 mg/day, increase by 12.5 mg every 3-5 days. Mean effective dose 3-4 mg/kg/day. Max 75 mg/day. Risk of depression, suicidality, parkinsonism, akathisia. Contraindicated if actively suicidal.

Tizanidine.duration_note: Titrate from bedtime dose over weeks
Tizanidine.notes: Start 1 mg at bedtime (ages 2-9) or 2 mg at bedtime (ages 10-17). Target 0.3-0.5 mg/kg/day TDS-QID. Max 24 mg/day. Monitor LFTs. Sedation is dose-limiting.

Topiramate.duration_note: Titrate by 1-3 mg/kg/day every 1-2 weeks over 5-7 weeks
Topiramate.notes: Start low, go slow. Start 0.5-1 mg/kg/day (or 25 mg) at bedtime. Increase by 1-3 mg/kg/day every 1-2 weeks.

Maintenance 5-9 mg/kg/day divided BD. Max 400 mg/day. Effective as monotherapy or adjunctive. Side effects: cognitive slowing, word-finding difficulty, weight loss, kidney stones, metabolic acidosis, glaucoma.
Start 0.5-1 mg/kg/day, titrate over 4-8 weeks to 2-3 mg/kg/day. Max 200 mg/day for prophylaxis. Ensure adequate hydration.

Trazodone.notes: Off-label for insomnia. Start 25-50 mg at bedtime. Usual 1-2 mg/kg. Median effective dose ~50 mg/day (range 25-200 mg). FDA BLACK BOX WARNING on suicidality with all antidepressants in <25y.

Trihexyphenidyl.notes: Start ~0.1 mg/kg/day. Increase by 10-20% every 2 weeks. Effective range 6-120 mg/day in divided doses. Mean max ~0.55 mg/kg/day. Anticholinergic side effects: dry mouth, blurred vision, constipation, urinary retention. Children tolerate higher doses than adults.

Valproate.duration_note: Titrate weekly to maintenance over 2-4 weeks
Valproate.notes: Start 10-15 mg/kg/day divided BD. Increase by 5-10 mg/kg/day weekly. Therapeutic level 50-100 mcg/mL.

Maintenance 20-40 mg/kg/day. Max 60 mg/kg/day. Therapeutic range 50-100 mcg/mL. Monitor LFT, CBC, ammonia, platelets. WARNING: hepatotoxicity risk highest in children <2y on polytherapy. Teratogenic — avoid in females of reproductive age.
IV loading 20-40 mg/kg over 10-15 min. Then maintenance 5 mg/kg q6-8h. Second-line agent for status epilepticus.
10-20 mg/kg/day BD for migraine prophylaxis. Effective for adolescents. CONTRAINDICATED in females of reproductive potential without effective contraception (teratogenic).
Infantile spasms: 20-100 mg/kg/day. May need higher doses. Max 150 mg/kg/day has been used. HIGH hepatotoxicity risk in <2y — monitor LFTs closely.

ACECLOFENAC+PARACETAMOL+SERRATIOPEPTIDAS.notes: Adult combination. NOT recommended for children <12y. Each tablet: aceclofenac 100 mg + paracetamol 325 mg + serratiopeptidase 15 mg. Give after food. Short course only. Avoid in renal impairment.

ALPRAZOLAM.duration_note: Short-term only; max 2 weeks. Taper before stopping.
ALPRAZOLAM.notes: NOT first-line. Off-label in children. 0.005-0.02 mg/kg TDS. Max 3.5 mg/day. Short-term use ONLY. Dependence risk. SSRIs preferred for chronic anxiety. Taper dose — never stop abruptly.

BUSPIRONE.duration_note: Takes 2-4 weeks for effect; reassess at 4 weeks
BUSPIRONE.notes: Off-label in children. Start 5 mg BD. Increase by 5 mg every 1-2 weeks. Mean optimal dose ~28 mg/day. Max 60 mg/day. No dependence risk unlike benzodiazepines. Did not separate from placebo in RCTs for pediatric GAD.

CLOBAZAM.notes: Weight <=30 kg: Start 5 mg OD, increase to 5 mg BD after 1 week, then 10 mg BD. Max 20 mg/day. FDA approved ages >=2 for LGS.
Weight >30 kg: Start 5 mg BD, increase to 10 mg BD after 1 week, then 20 mg BD. Max 40 mg/day. Target 0.25-1 mg/kg/day. Tolerance may develop; higher doses up to 2 mg/kg/day used off-label.

IBUPROFEN+PARACETAMOL.duration_note: Short-term only; max 3 days for combination
IBUPROFEN+PARACETAMOL.notes: Dose based on ibuprofen component: 5-10 mg/kg/dose q6-8h. Common Indian FDC: ibuprofen 100 mg + paracetamol 162.5 mg per 5 mL. Not first-line — use single agents when possible. Short-term use only.

MEFENAMIC ACID.notes: 4-6.5 mg/kg/dose TDS. Max 3 doses/day. Give after food. Suspension available for children <12y. Not first-line; paracetamol and ibuprofen preferred. Can cause diarrhea.

MEFENAMIC ACID+PARACETAMOL.notes: Dose based on mefenamic acid component: 4-6.5 mg/kg TDS. Common FDC: mefenamic acid 50 mg + paracetamol 125 mg per 5 mL. Avoid total paracetamol >60 mg/kg/day. Short-term use only.

NAPROXEN+DOMPERIDONE.notes: Adult combination. NOT for children <12y. Typical tablet: naproxen 250 mg + domperidone 10 mg. Domperidone: cardiac QTc risk — avoid with QT-prolonging drugs. Max naproxen 500 mg/day in this formulation.

NEOSTIGMINE.notes: 0.03-0.07 mg/kg IV (max 5 mg). ALWAYS give with atropine (0.02 mg/kg) or glycopyrrolate (0.01 mg/kg) to prevent bradycardia. Drug of choice for NMB reversal in pediatrics.

NEOSTIGMINE.duration_note: Ongoing for MG; adjust to clinical response
NEOSTIGMINE.notes: IM/SC: 0.04 mg/kg q2-4h. Oral not available in India; pyridostigmine preferred for chronic MG management.

PREGABALIN+METHYLCOBALAMIN.notes: NOT recommended in children <16y. Adult FDC: pregabalin 75 mg + methylcobalamin 750 mcg (or 1500 mcg). Max pregabalin 600 mg/day. Dizziness, somnolence common. Dose reduce in renal impairment.

TRAMADOL.notes: FDA CONTRAINDICATED in children <12y. Avoid in 12-18y with obesity/OSA/severe lung disease. CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers at risk of fatal respiratory depression. Use morphine or other opioids instead. If used in adolescents >=12y: 1-2 mg/kg q4-6h, max 400 mg/day. NEVER for post-tonsillectomy pain.

TRYPSIN-CHYMOTRYPSIN.notes: Not for children <12y. Proteolytic enzyme combination used as anti-inflammatory adjunct. Limited pediatric evidence. Take on empty stomach for systemic effect.

Almotriptan.duration_note: Single dose; may repeat after 2h. Max 2 doses/24h
Almotriptan.notes: FDA approved ages >=12 for acute migraine. 6.25-12.5 mg at onset; may repeat after 2h. Max 25 mg/24h. Not for prophylaxis.

Ataluren.notes: 40 mg/kg/day divided: 10 mg/kg AM, 10 mg/kg midday, 20 mg/kg evening. Doses at least 6h apart. EMA-approved for ambulant patients >=2y with nonsense mutation DMD. Mix granules in food/liquid.

Benztropine.notes: 0.02-0.05 mg/kg/dose IV/IM (max 2 mg). For acute drug-induced dystonia. Not for children <3y. Continue oral for 48-72h to prevent recurrence. Anticholinergic side effects.

Cenobamate.notes: Off-label in children. Start 12.5 mg OD (~0.2 mg/kg/day). Titrate slowly: increase by ~0.5 mg/kg/day every 2 weeks. Target 2-5 mg/kg/day (<=6y may need ~5 mg/kg/day; adolescents ~2.7 mg/kg/day). Max 400 mg/day or 4.5 mg/kg/day for <50 kg. SLOW titration mandatory — risk of DRESS syndrome.

Dantrolene.duration_note: Titrate weekly; ongoing with LFT monitoring
Dantrolene.notes: Start 0.5 mg/kg OD. Increase weekly to max 12 mg/kg/day or 400 mg/day (whichever less) in 4 divided doses. BLACK BOX WARNING: hepatotoxicity — monitor LFTs. Avoid long-term use if possible. Also used for malignant hyperthermia (IV: 2.5 mg/kg, repeat to 10 mg/kg).

Dantrolene.duration_note: Emergency use; repeat until MH resolves
Dantrolene.notes: IV: 2.5 mg/kg initial bolus, repeat every 5-10 min (cumulative max 10 mg/kg). Continue oral 1-2 mg/kg QID for 24-48h post-crisis. Life-saving in MH.

Eculizumab.duration_note: Induction: weekly x1, then maintenance every 3 weeks
Eculizumab.notes: 5-<10 kg: 300 mg week 1, then 300 mg week 2, then 300 mg q3 weeks. Requires meningococcal vaccination >=2 weeks before first dose. IV infusion over 1-4 hours in children.

Eculizumab.duration_note: Induction: 600 mg week 1, then 300 mg week 2, then 300 mg q2 weeks
Eculizumab.notes: 10-<20 kg: Induction 600 mg week 1, then 300 mg week 2. Maintenance 300 mg q2 weeks. Meningococcal vaccination required.

Eculizumab.duration_note: Induction: 600 mg weekly x2, then 600 mg q2 weeks
Eculizumab.notes: 20-<30 kg: Induction 600 mg weekly x2 doses. Maintenance 600 mg q2 weeks.

Fosphenytoin.notes: 15-20 mg PE/kg IV at 1-2 mg PE/kg/min (max 150 mg PE/min). PE = phenytoin equivalents. 1.5 mg fosphenytoin = 1 mg PE. Second-line for SE after benzodiazepines. Monitor ECG during infusion.

Fosphenytoin.duration_note: Start 12h after loading; switch to oral phenytoin when possible
Fosphenytoin.notes: Maintenance 4-8 mg PE/kg/day IV divided BD. Start 12h after loading dose. Target phenytoin level 10-20 mcg/mL. Transition to oral phenytoin when feasible.

Glycerin suppositories.duration_note: As needed; not for daily use
Glycerin suppositories.notes: Infant size (1 g) suppository: insert rectally PRN. Works within 15-60 min. Can cut adult suppository in half for infants. Not for regular daily use.

Glycerin suppositories.duration_note: As needed; not for daily use
Glycerin suppositories.notes: Child (2 g) or adult (3 g) suppository rectally PRN. Works 15-60 min. Osmotic laxative. For occasional use; address underlying cause.

Guanfacine.notes: Start 1 mg/day at bedtime or late PM. Increase by 1 mg/week. Target 0.05-0.12 mg/kg/day. Max 4 mg/day for ages 6-12. Do NOT stop abruptly — rebound hypertension.
Start 1 mg/day. Max 7 mg/day for ages 13-17. Can be used as monotherapy or adjunct to stimulants.

Guanfacine ER.notes: Intuniv (guanfacine ER). Start 1 mg/day, increase by 1 mg/week. 6-12y: max 4 mg. 13-17y: max 7 mg. Swallow whole — do NOT crush/chew. Give at bedtime or morning. Taper by 1 mg q3-7 days when stopping.

Omaveloxolone.notes: FDA approved >=16y. 150 mg OD on empty stomach (1h before or 2h after food). Pediatric dosing for <16y not yet established (trials ongoing). Monitor BNP/LFTs. Avoid strong/moderate CYP3A4 inhibitors.

Polyethylene Glycol 3350 (MiraLAX).notes: Start 0.5 g/kg/day; titrate to effect (max 1.5 g/kg/day or 17 g/day). 1 capful = 17 g. Dissolve in 8 oz (240 mL) water/juice. Safe for long-term use. Tasteless, odorless.

Polyethylene Glycol 3350 (MiraLAX).duration_note: 3-day course; then switch to maintenance dose
Polyethylene Glycol 3350 (MiraLAX).notes: 1-1.5 g/kg/day for 3 days for disimpaction. 95% effective at higher dose. Then step down to maintenance 0.5-0.8 g/kg/day.

Senna (Sennosides).notes: Ages 2-6: 4.4-6.6 mg sennosides at bedtime. Max 6.6 mg BD. Syrup: 8.8 mg/5 mL. Stimulant laxative — works in 6-12h. Second-line after osmotic laxatives. Not for daily long-term use without supervision.
Ages 6-12: 1 tablet (8.6 mg sennosides) at bedtime. Max 2 tablets (17.2 mg) daily.

Eteplirsen.notes: 30 mg/kg IV once weekly over 35-60 min. FDA approved for DMD amenable to exon 51 skipping. Studied in boys >=7y; safety data available from 6 months of age. Monitor renal function. Specialist only.

Ethosuximide.notes: Ages 3-6: Start 125 mg BD (250 mg/day). Increase by 250 mg every 4-7 days. First-line for childhood absence epilepsy (equal to valproate, superior to lamotrigine).

Maintenance 20 mg/kg/day optimal; up to 40 mg/kg/day for refractory cases. Max 1500 mg/day (some use up to 2000 mg). Therapeutic level 40-100 mcg/mL. Syrup 250 mg/5 mL. Monitor CBC (rare blood dyscrasias).

Mannitol.duration_note: May repeat q6-8h; monitor serum osmolality
Mannitol.notes: 20% mannitol: 0.25-1 g/kg IV over 20-30 min. For acute ICP crisis: 0.5-1 g/kg over 15 min. May repeat q6-8h. STOP if serum osmolality >320 mOsm or electrolyte derangement. Can worsen cerebral edema with repeated doses if BBB is disrupted.

Mannitol.duration_note: Ongoing as needed; monitor osmolality q4-6h
Mannitol.notes: 0.25-0.5 g/kg q6h. Monitor serum osmolality q4-6h, renal function, electrolytes. Insert Foley catheter (osmotic diuresis). Consider hypertonic saline 3% as alternative.

Valbenazine.notes: Off-label in pediatrics. Start 40 mg/day, may increase to 80 mg/day after 1 week. Mean pediatric dose ~1.3 mg/kg/day. FDA approved only for adults with tardive dyskinesia. Monitor for depression, suicidality, QT prolongation.

NALBUPHINE.notes: 0.1-0.2 mg/kg IV/IM/SC q3-4h PRN. Max 20 mg/dose. Mixed agonist-antagonist opioid. Ceiling effect for respiratory depression (safer than morphine). Not for infants <1y (safety not established).

PIROXICAM.notes: 0.3-0.5 mg/kg OD. Max 20 mg/day. Long half-life (50h) — once daily dosing. Target plasma level 3-5 mcg/mL. Higher GI bleeding risk than other NSAIDs. Not first-line in children.

IV methylprednisolone.duration_note: 3 consecutive days per pulse; cycles as per protocol
IV methylprednisolone.notes: 15-30 mg/kg/day (max 1000 mg) IV over 1-2h for 3 days. Indications: epileptic encephalopathy, acute demyelination, lupus nephritis, nephrotic syndrome relapse, ADEM. Monitor BP, glucose, potassium during infusion.

IV methylprednisolone.indication: Acute spinal cord injury / Severe inflammation
IV methylprednisolone.duration_note: Single dose or 3-day course per specialist protocol
IV methylprednisolone.notes: Dose varies by condition: lupus nephritis 30 mg/kg (max 1g) x3 days; MS relapse 20-30 mg/kg (max 1g) x3-5 days; nephrotic relapse 10-15 mg/kg (max 1g) x3 days alternate day.

Intrathecal baclofen.duration_note: Single bolus test dose; observe for 4-8h
Intrathecal baclofen.notes: Screening: start 25-50 mcg IT bolus. Observe 4-8h for spasticity reduction. If no response, may try 75 mcg then 100 mcg at 24h intervals. Non-responders at 100 mcg are not candidates for pump.

Intrathecal baclofen.duration_note: Continuous IT infusion; lifelong with pump refills
Intrathecal baclofen.notes: Initial daily dose = 2x effective screening dose (or 1x if effect lasted >8h). Titrate by 5-15% per day. Maintenance range: 90-703 mcg/day (typical). Max 1400 mcg/day. Abrupt withdrawal is life-threatening (fever, seizures, rhabdomyolysis). Specialist only.

ACECLOFENAC.notes: NOT recommended for children <12y (limited pediatric data). Adult dose: 100 mg BD after food. Use shortest duration. Avoid in renal impairment, GI ulcer history. Paracetamol/ibuprofen preferred in pediatrics.

PENTAZOCINE.notes: 0.5 mg/kg IM/IV q6-8h. Ages 5-8y: 15 mg; ages >=9y: 30 mg. Mixed agonist-antagonist. May precipitate withdrawal in opioid-dependent patients. Limited pediatric data. Injection may be discontinued in some markets.
